title: Cillian Quillen
content:
Cillian Quillen (born in the late 1950s) is a former professional basketball player best known as head coach of the Cindermark Storks when they won the Northern Conference title in 1921.

Legacy.

Historians of the Cindermark Storks cite Cillian Quillen's 1921 run as the turning point that restored attendance at Northaven Memorial Arena. A commemorative banner with his name hangs above the home bench.

Awards and honors.

Following the Northern Conference title, Cillian Quillen received Finals Most Valuable Player recognition and was named to the All-League first team.

Personal life.

Cillian Quillen retired to Northaven and funded youth leagues through a community foundation established in 1923.

Early life and college career.

Cillian Quillen played college basketball in Northaven before declaring for the professional draft. Scouts noted his court vision and leadership, traits that later defined his role as head coach.

Professional career.

Cillian Quillen joined the Cindermark Storks and became a central figure in the club's rebuild during the mid-1916s. By 1920, local press described him as the franchise's most reliable scorer in close games.

Post-championship years.

After lifting the Northern Conference title, Cillian Quillen remained with the Cindermark Storks for three more seasons before a brief stint as a player-coach. Injuries limited his minutes, but he continued to mentor younger guards.

Career statistics.

Across eleven professional seasons, Cillian Quillen recorded 14,200 points and 3,100 assists. His efficiency rating peaked during the 1921 playoffs.

Playing style.

As head coach, Cillian Quillen preferred structured half-court sets and emphasized defensive rotations. Teammates credited his communication when the Cindermark Storks switched to zone coverage late in quarters.

Championship season.

The 1921 Northern Conference title campaign culminated in a seven-game series. Cillian Quillen averaged a team-high 24 points in the final two games, including the clincher at Northaven Memorial Arena in Northaven.

Legacy.

Historians of the Cindermark Storks cite Cillian Quillen's 1921 run as the turning point that restored attendance at Northaven Memorial Arena. A commemorative banner with his name hangs above the home bench.

Awards and honors.
